metadata.title: International Trade and Policy
learning_objectives: Explain comparative advantage and gains from trade
Derive excess demand and supply functions
Analyze free trade vs autarky welfare effects
Calculate tariff impacts on prices and welfare

questions.question: Vietnam: 5 hrs/rice, 10 hrs/textile. Japan: 2 hrs/rice, 3 hrs/textile. Find absolute and comparative advantages.
hints: Absolute advantage: fewer resources to produce

questions.question: Using Problem 1 countries, find mutually beneficial ToT for textiles.
Qᴰ = 120 - 2p, Qˢ = 3p - 30, pʷ = 24. Find autarky eq, trade direction, and welfare changes.
Same market (Qᴰ=120-2p, Qˢ=3p-30, pʷ=24), tariff t=6. Full welfare analysis.
Same market, import quota M̄ = 12. Find price, quantities, and quota rent.
Qᴰ = 80-p, Qˢ = 2p-10, pʷ = 40. Analyze export tax (t=9) and subsidy (s=6).
Qᴰ = 200-4p, Qˢ = 2p-40, foreign export supply X* = 4pʷ-80. Analyze tariff t=10.
hints: Large country: domestic price ≠ world price + t

questions.question: For the large country in W10-P7, find the optimal tariff.
Australian beef: Qᴰ = 1200-10p, Qˢ = 5p+200, export demand Qᵉˣᵖ = 800-5pʷ. Analyze Japanese tariff.
Compare tariff, quota, and production subsidy for reducing imports by 50%.
Qᴰ = a - bp, Qˢ = c + dp. Derive excess demand ED(p).
Write R functions for trade analysis. Test with Qᴰ=100-2p, Qˢ=p-20, pʷ=30, t=10.
